entries.label: The following is the authorized initial manpower and equipment
Contractor:
Personnel:
Response Vehicles:
Scope of Work:
Location of Services:
Lewis Contact:
Signature:
Cell Phone:
Email:
Job#/PO#
Date:

A commitment to ensuring the safety of network employees is a requirement and critical priority of Lewis Environmental and all subcontractors. 

Before the start of any services a job safety analysis shall be performed.  During any change in the scope of work or the appearance of new hazards; work should be stopped and safe work practices should be re-addressed. 

All employees are required to wear the following personal protective equipment (PPE) when on any project site.  Hard hat, safety glasses, gloves and steel/composite toe boots. 

The subcontractor performed safety briefing must be included in the invoice package.

The contractor will immediately contact Lewis when they arrive at the site.  Contractor will send digital photographs upon arrival.  Once the initial release has been contained; the contractor must discuss the scope of work before initiating any remedial action.   

Contractor must verbally update every hour regarding all aspects of the operations.

NOTE- BEFORE, DURING AND AFTER PHOTOS MUST BE PROVIDED TO THE LEWIS ENVIRONMENTAL INCIDENT MANAGER VIA TEXT OR EMAIL THROUGHOUT RESPONSE.  

EACH PHOTO MUST BE PROVIDED WITH GPS LOCATIONS.

Waste material will remain onsite in an area designated by the client representative; unless otherwise authorized by Lewis.  It is imperative that the exact size/volume, quantity and contents of waste containers be reported. 

Any waste transportation paperwork, profiles or facilities to be utilized for bulk liquid wastes; must be reviewed by Lewis prior to disposal activities.
LEWIS ENVIRONMENTAL IS NEVER THE GENERATOR OF WASTE
Immediately following the clean-up activities (no more than 24 hours after completion), contractor will forward the following information to the RC.

•	Pictures of site conditions prior to clean-up or remedial action
•	Pictures of the clean-up or remedial action
•	Picture of the site conditions at the end of the services
•	Pictures of contractor equipment used onsite.
•	Field notes indicating hours worked, chronological timeline of work performed, equipment utilized, materials & PPE, etc.
•	Waste Tracking form
•	Site Map (indicating size, sample and field screening locations; as required)

All aspects of this project and photographs taken are considered CONFIDENTIAL.  No information may be released by the contractor to any party, including government/regulatory, agencies without the explicit consent of Lewis.

t is expressly understood that CONTRACTOR is an independent CONTRACTOR and that neither it nor its employees or subcontractors or their respective employees are servants, agents or employees of Lewis Environmental, Inc.   

The actual performance and superintendence of all work shall be by CONTRACTOR, and under the control and director of CONTRACTOR as to the details of the work; provided, however, Lewis, being interested in the results to be obtained, is authorized to designate a representative, who shall at all times have access to the location where the work is to be performed for the purposes of observing and inspecting.
Modifications made to this authorization, must only be made by Lewis.  Any changes made by the contractor will not be permitted.

LEWIS ENVIRONMENTAL DOES NOT ACCEPT/PERMIT CHARGES FOR THE FOLLOWING:  

-	Administrative Charges/Clerical Charges 
        unless authorized in writing from Lewis 
         Project Manager.
-	Project Manager Charges
-	Cellular Phones and Cameras
-	Hand Tool Charges require receipts for 
        replacement

Additional Personnel or Equipment added to the response will not be authorized for invoicing without written approval from a Lewis Environmental Manager.  

A copy of this authorization must be submitted with the contractor invoice. 

Failure to follow requirements will result in delayed payment.  No payment will be made for unauthorized work and/or costs. 

By signing this contractor work authorization, the contractor acknowledges that they have read and understand the conditions set above and set previously in written network communication.  
The contractor acknowledges that the requirements have been accepted.
